dict.string: Pan-Agent uses Apple Events to drive scriptable apps you choose (Finder, Mail, Calendar, etc.) when you ask the agent to perform tasks in them.
Pan-Agent captures your screen so the visual tools can see what you see and act on it. Captures stay on your machine and are HMAC-redacted before being journaled.
Pan-Agent uses Accessibility APIs to read window titles and click UI elements when you ask the agent to drive other applications.
Pan-Agent may request Full Disk Access lazily — only when an action you've authorized needs to write to a TCC-protected location (e.g., ~/Library, ~/Desktop).
